buggy_code:
from zipline.api import schedule_function, date_rules, time_rules
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge
import numpy as np

def initialize(context):
    context.model = Ridge()
    context.model_trained = False
    # Only train once at the start — never retrain
    schedule_function(train_model, date_rules.month_start(), time_rules.market_open())

def train_model(context, data):
    if not context.model_trained:
        X, y = get_training_data(context)
        context.model.fit(X, y)
        context.model_trained = True
    # After first training, never updates the model

def rebalance(context, data):
    X = get_current_features(context, data)
    pred = context.model.predict(X)
clean_code:
from zipline.api import schedule_function, date_rules, time_rules
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge

def initialize(context):
    context.model = Ridge()
    context.training_window = 252  # 1 year of data
    # Retrain monthly
    schedule_function(train_model, date_rules.month_start(), time_rules.market_open())
    schedule_function(rebalance, date_rules.month_start(), time_rules.market_open(minutes=5))

def train_model(context, data):
    # Always retrain with latest data
    X, y = get_training_data(context, window=context.training_window)
    context.model.fit(X, y)

def rebalance(context, data):
    X = get_current_features(context, data)
    pred = context.model.predict(X)
description: Model trained only once at initialization and never updated as market conditions change.
explanation: A model trained on data from 2015 may have learned patterns that no longer apply in 2022. Factor loadings, volatility regimes, and market microstructure all change over time. A static model becomes increasingly stale and its predictions increasingly unreliable. This is model staleness — a logic error in the retraining schedule.
suggested_fix: Retrain the model periodically (monthly or quarterly) using a rolling window of recent data.

issues.title: Model trained only once and never updated as market evolves
